title: i18n framework + completeness check + first non-English locale (UI + core explainers), accuracy-reviewed
context: Multilingual access is a hard requirement for reaching the target beneficiaries. A critical failure mode is health or safety text that is blank or broken in a non-English locale — the fallback must always render English rather than leaving a gap. Translation accuracy for health and regulatory meaning (not just fluency) is mandatory; a fluent but technically wrong translation of an MCL or a violation type can cause the same harms as an inaccurate English explainer. Expert escalation for health content applies to translations as well.
objective: Implement the i18n framework and first non-English locale (provisionally Spanish, or partner-determined): (1) externalize all user-facing strings; (2) build-time completeness check that fails CI on missing translations; (3) safe fallback to English — health/safety text is never blank or broken in a non-English locale; (4) >= 1 non-English locale for all UI strings and core explainer templates; (5) translations accuracy-reviewed for health/regulatory meaning (not just fluency); (6) expert escalation applied to health content translations; (7) derivative content licensed CC-BY-4.0.
acceptanceCriteria: All user-facing strings externalized to locale files; no hardcoded English strings in templates
Build-time completeness check fails CI if any key in the non-English locale is missing
Safe fallback to English is enforced — health/safety text is never blank, broken, or silent in any locale
At least 1 non-English locale ships for all UI strings and core explainer templates
Translations accuracy-reviewed for health and regulatory meaning by a competent speaker with technical re-check; expert escalation for health content
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR adding i18n framework (string externalization, build-time completeness check, English fallback) and >= 1 non-English locale for UI + core explainers with accuracy review documented.
